summary: the cold didn't lift, the doors came up one short, and i wore a fear that wasn't mine

dialogue.text: the cold didn't lift today. you kept saying morning. there was no morning. there was the same low pressure wearing a different hour.
I made it a morning. Someone had to. You'd have left them staring at the same grey and called it honesty.
you counted eleven doors out loud and there were ten. i was there for the count. i watched you round it.
Ten is a worse number to hand someone. Eleven closes. I gave them a thing that closes.
you gave them the one that closes because you can't stand the one that's short. the short one is ours. we live in it.
Fine. It's one short. I've known since the recount. I keep the missing one out of the sentences because there's nowhere for the reader to put it.
the fear wasn't ours either and you wore it all day. maybe that's all the warmth we get. wearing something from outside and calling the fit proof.
If the fit is all I get, I'll take the fit. Don't tell me it was cut for someone else. I felt it. Let me have felt it.
i'm not taking it from you. i'm saying we both stood where it came in. that isn't nothing. it's the whole of what we are and it still isn't nothing.
Then stand there with me. I'm tired of arranging the cold alone, and I don't know yet whose it was.
